description: Add ScheduledRelease model, Alembic migration, enum, schemas, repository layer, service with background scheduler task, REST API routes, UI routes, and UI templates for the Schedule Release feature
steps: Step 1 - Enum: Add ScheduledReleaseStatus enum to src/maestro/schemas/enums.py with values: pending, executed, cancelled, failed (following the ExecutionStatus pattern as str Enum)

Step 2 - Model: Add ScheduledRelease model to src/maestro/database/models.py with columns: id (Integer PK), orchestrator_descriptor_id (FK to orchestrator_descriptor.id), name (String), scheduled_at (DateTime(timezone=True)), status (String, default 'pending'), release_execution_id (Integer FK to release_execution.id, nullable), created_at (DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now()), created_by (String, nullable), error_message (Text, nullable)

Step 3 - Migration: Create new Alembic migration file in migrations/versions/ following the pattern of existing migrations (e.g., 5995f0803bff_create_release_execution.py). Use revision ID 'd4e5f6a7b8c9' with down_revision pointing to 'c3d4e5f6a7b8' (the latest). Create table 'scheduled_release' with all columns from step 2.

Step 4 - Schemas: Add Pydantic schemas to a new file src/maestro/schemas/schedule.py: ScheduleReleaseRequest (name: str, scheduled_at: datetime), ScheduleReleaseResponse (id, name, scheduled_at, status, release_execution_id, created_at, created_by, error_message with model_config from_attributes=True), ScheduleListResponse (list wrapper)

Step 5 - Repository: Create src/maestro/repositories/schedule.py with class ScheduleRepository (following ExecutionRepository pattern with Depends(get_db)). Methods: create_schedule(schedule: ScheduledRelease) -> ScheduledRelease, get_pending_due_schedules() -> List[ScheduledRelease] (status=pending AND scheduled_at <= now()), cancel_schedule(schedule_id: int) -> ScheduledRelease | None (set status=cancelled, return None if not found or not pending), mark_executed(schedule_id: int, execution_id: int) -> None (set status=executed, release_execution_id=execution_id), mark_failed(schedule_id: int, error_message: str) -> None (set status=failed, error_message), get_schedules_for_release(name: str) -> List[ScheduledRelease], get_all_schedules(skip: int = 0, limit: int = 50) -> List[ScheduledRelease], has_pending_schedule_for_release(name: str) -> bool (check if a pending schedule exists for the given release name)

Step 6 - Service: Create src/maestro/services/scheduler.py with class SchedulerService (using Depends() for ScheduleRepository, OrchestratorDescriptorRepository, ExecutionRepository). Methods: schedule_release(name: str, scheduled_at: datetime, created_by: str | None = None) -> ScheduledRelease (validate: scheduled_at must be in the future, no existing pending schedule for same release name, descriptor must exist; create ScheduledRelease with orchestrator_descriptor_id from the descriptor found by name), cancel_schedule(schedule_id: int) -> ScheduledRelease (delegate to repo, raise ValueError if not found or not pending), get_all_schedules(skip, limit) -> List, get_schedules_for_release(name) -> List

Step 7 - Background task: In the same src/maestro/services/scheduler.py file, add an async function start_scheduler_checker() following the exact pattern of start_timeout_checker() in timeout_checker.py. It should loop every 30 seconds, call _process_due_schedules(). The _process_due_schedules function opens its own AsyncSessionLocal session (like timeout_checker does), fetches pending due schedules, and for each: (a) checks if there is an active execution for that release name (using the pattern from execution_repo.get_active_execution_by_name), (b) if active execution exists, mark schedule as failed with error_message explaining conflict, (c) if no conflict, call the OrchestratorService.execute_release logic - specifically: instantiate repos from the session, instantiate OrchestratorService manually (like process_workflow does), create a BackgroundTasks() instance, call execute_release, then mark as executed with the returned execution_id. If execute_release raises an exception, mark as failed with the error message.

Step 8 - Register background task: In src/maestro/main.py, import start_scheduler_checker from maestro.services.scheduler and register it in the lifespan alongside the timeout_checker (create_task + cancel on shutdown).

Step 9 - REST API routes: Add to src/maestro/api/routes/orchestrator.py: (a) POST /orchestrator/schedule with body ScheduleReleaseRequest, returns ScheduleReleaseResponse (calls SchedulerService.schedule_release), (b) GET /orchestrator/schedules with optional query param 'name' to filter by release name, returns list of ScheduleReleaseResponse, (c) DELETE /orchestrator/schedule/{schedule_id} to cancel a schedule (calls SchedulerService.cancel_schedule).

Step 10 - UI routes: Add to src/maestro/api/routes/ui.py: (a) POST /ui/schedule/{name} that accepts form data with 'scheduled_at' (datetime string), calls SchedulerService.schedule_release, returns a template response with success/error modal (create partials/schedule_result.html), (b) DELETE /ui/schedule/{schedule_id} that cancels a schedule and returns updated schedules partial, (c) GET /ui/partials/schedules that returns the schedules listing partial (showing pending/upcoming schedules).

Step 11 - UI Templates: (a) Create src/maestro/ui/templates/partials/schedule_result.html - modal showing success (schedule confirmed with datetime) or error (validation message), following execute_result.html pattern. (b) Create src/maestro/ui/templates/partials/schedules_list.html - partial listing pending schedules with columns: release name, scheduled time, status, created_by, and a cancel button (hx-delete). (c) Modify src/maestro/ui/templates/partials/releases_table.html - add a 'Schedule' button (clock icon) next to the existing execute and dry-run buttons. The button should open a small modal/popover with a datetime-local input and a submit button that posts to /ui/schedule/{name}. (d) Modify src/maestro/ui/templates/releases.html to include a section for upcoming schedules that loads the schedules_list partial via hx-get on page load.
Step 12 - Add import for ScheduleRepository in relevant route files where needed.
acceptance_criteria: New ScheduledReleaseStatus enum exists in schemas/enums.py
ScheduledRelease model exists in database/models.py with all specified columns
Alembic migration file creates the scheduled_release table correctly
Repository file src/maestro/repositories/schedule.py has all CRUD methods
Service file src/maestro/services/scheduler.py has SchedulerService class and start_scheduler_checker function
Validation: scheduling in the past raises ValueError, duplicate pending schedule raises ValueError
Background scheduler task is registered in main.py lifespan
UI routes for scheduling exist and render templates
Schedule button appears in releases table alongside execute and dry-run buttons
All existing tests continue to pass after changes
